home.title: Carwoods — Houston rentals, managed right
home.metaDescription: Houston rentals and property management: clear applications, consistent communication, on-time rent collection, proactive maintenance, and disciplined asset care.
home.heroSubtitle: Clear applications. Consistent communication. On-time rent collection, proactive maintenance, and disciplined asset care.
home.heroImageAlt: Keys to a new home resting on a welcome mat
home.midHeroAlt: Modern home exterior with warm evening light
home.highlight1Detail: Local leasing rhythms, not generic playbooks
home.highlight2Detail: Applications, rent, and maintenance in one flow
home.rentersBody: Looking for a lease in Houston? We keep the path honest: what you need to apply, what to expect, and people who pick up the phone.
home.rentersBullet1: Streamlined application steps and required-documents guidance
home.rentersBullet2: Local listings mindset—we know this market's rhythms
home.rentersBullet3: Respect for your time: fewer surprises, clearer next steps
home.ownersBody: Hand us the keys to the busywork. We market vacancies, screen tenants, coordinate repairs, and report back so you can stay focused on the big picture.
home.ownersBullet1: Full-service property management tailored to your goals
home.ownersBullet3: Steady communication—you're never guessing what's happening

apply.title: Carwoods - How to Apply
apply.metaDescription: How to apply to rent a Carwoods property. Review criteria, gather documents, and submit your rental application online.
apply.heading: How to Apply to Rent
apply.intro: All the information you need to apply is on this site. No need to call — follow the steps below. When you finish reviewing eligibility or the documents list, use Back to How to Apply at the top of those pages to return here and continue with step 3.
apply.step2BodySuffix: list so your application is complete.
apply.step3Body: Choose a property below to start the online application. Each card also links to the full listing for photos and details.
apply.listingsEmpty: No rental listings are available to show right now.
apply.listingsUnavailable: We could not load the latest listings. Please try again later.
apply.fullListingDetailsAria: Full listing details for {{addressLine}} (opens in new tab)
apply.applyPhotoAria: Apply for {{addressLine}} — opens rental application (new tab)

contact.title: Carwoods - Contact Us
contact.metaDescription: Start with our Apply page for leasing steps and documents. Contact our HAR.com agent when you need direct help after following that process.
contact.heading: Contact Us
contact.para1Prefix: For rental applications and most leasing-related questions, please begin with our
contact.para1Suffix: page. It explains eligibility, required documentation, and how to submit through the listing channel. Following that workflow first allows us to assist you efficiently and consistently.
contact.para2: If you have already worked through those steps, or you have a matter that cannot be addressed there—such as property management services or another topic that requires direct outreach—please contact our licensed agent on HAR.com (Houston Association of Realtors). We encourage using this channel when self-service options are not sufficient.
contact.ctaAriaLabel: Contact our agent on HAR.com (opens in new tab)

privacy.title: Carwoods - Privacy Policy
privacy.metaDescription: Carwoods privacy policy — how we collect, use, and protect your information.
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Carwoods ("we," "our," or "us") is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy describes how we handle information when you use our website and services.
privacy.collectBody: When you visit our site, we may collect information you provide directly, such as when you contact us or submit an application. If you use third-party services (e.g., HAR.com for property inquiries or applications), their privacy policies govern that data.
privacy.useBody: We use information to respond to inquiries, process applications, provide property management services, and comply with legal obligations. We do not sell your personal information.
privacy.thirdPartyBody: Our site may link to external services (e.g., Texas Real Estate Commission, HAR.com). We are not responsible for their privacy practices. Please review their policies before sharing information.
privacy.notificationsBody: You may control many portal notification settings in your profile, including email, in-app, and SMS preferences. Security and compliance notices may still be sent when required by law, policy, or account protection needs.
privacy.contactBody: For privacy-related questions or to request access to your information, contact us through our

terms.title: Carwoods - Terms of Service
terms.metaDescription: Carwoods terms of service for website use, listing information, third-party tools, and user responsibilities.
terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your use of the Carwoods website and related services for renters, applicants, landlords, and property owners. By using this site, you agree to these Terms.
terms.useOfSiteBody: You may use this site to review rental information, application guidance, and property management services for lawful personal or business purposes. You agree not to misuse the site, attempt unauthorized access, or interfere with site operations.
terms.listingsBody: We work to keep listing details, pricing, and availability current, but information can change without notice. The site content is provided for general information and does not guarantee that any property will remain available or that any application will be approved.
terms.thirdPartyBody: Some actions may route you to third-party services such as HAR.com or other application providers. Those services have their own terms, policies, and availability. We are not responsible for third-party content, downtime, or actions outside our control.
terms.eligibilityBody: Submitting an inquiry, rental application, or owner contact request does not create a lease, management agreement, or promise of acceptance. Final decisions are made after required screening, documentation review, service fit evaluation, and compliance checks under applicable laws and company standards.
terms.prohibitedUseBody: You may not copy, scrape, republish, or commercially exploit site content without written permission. You also may not upload malicious code, submit false information, or use this site in a way that violates law or another person's rights.
terms.liabilityBody: This site is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, to the extent allowed by law. Carwoods is not liable for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from your use of the site or reliance on posted information.
terms.legalNoticeBody: Information on this site is provided for general informational purposes only and is not legal, tax, accounting, or investment advice. You should consult qualified professionals for advice specific to your situation.
terms.changesBody: We may update these Terms from time to time. Updated Terms are effective when posted on this page. Your continued use of the site after updates means you accept the revised Terms.
terms.notificationsBody: By using portal messaging features, you agree to receive service notifications related to onboarding and maintenance activity according to your profile preferences. SMS delivery requires explicit opt-in. Security and compliance notifications may be sent regardless of optional channel preferences.
terms.contactBody: If you have questions about these Terms, please contact us through our

accessibility.title: Carwoods - Accessibility Statement
accessibility.metaDescription: Carwoods commitment to digital accessibility and WCAG compliance.
accessibility.heading: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.intro: Carwoods is committed to ensuring our website is accessible to people with disabilities. We aim to comply with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 at Level AA.
accessibility.measure1: Semantic HTML and clear heading structure
accessibility.measure2: Keyboard navigable content with visible focus indicators
accessibility.measure4: Color contrast that meets WCAG guidelines
accessibility.measure5: A skip link to main content for keyboard users
accessibility.feedbackBody: If you encounter any accessibility barriers on our site, please contact us through our
accessibility.feedbackBodySuffix: page. We will work to address the issue promptly.

propertyManagement.title: Carwoods - Property Management
propertyManagement.heading: Property Management
propertyManagement.s1Body: The Owner appoints the Property Manager as the exclusive agent to rent, lease, operate, and manage the Property.
propertyManagement.s2Body: This Agreement shall commence on [Start Date] and continue until [End Date] unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms herein.
propertyManagement.s3Body: The Owner agrees to pay the Property Manager a management fee of 8% of the monthly rental income. Additional fees may apply for specific services as outlined in Section 4.
propertyManagement.s4Intro: The Property Manager agrees to perform the following services:
propertyManagement.s4Li1: Marketing and advertising the Property for rent.
propertyManagement.s4Li8: Providing annual financial statements to the Owner.
propertyManagement.s4Li9: Providing monthly financial statements to the Owner upon request.
propertyManagement.s5Intro: The Owner agrees to reimburse the Property Manager for all reasonable expenses incurred in the management of the Property, including but not limited to:
propertyManagement.s5Li3: Legal fees for lease enforcement and eviction processes.
propertyManagement.s6Body1: The Owner agrees to establish and maintain a reserve fund of $1,000 to be held by the Property Manager. This fund is to be used for emergency repairs, unforeseen expenses, and any other expenses necessary for the upkeep and maintenance of the property that are not covered by the regular operating budget. The Property Manager is authorized to use this reserve fund as needed to cover such expenses.
propertyManagement.s6Body2: The Property Manager shall notify the Owner promptly when the reserve fund falls below the specified amount. The reserve fund will be replenished from the rental income collected by the Property Manager until it reaches the $1,000 level. The Property Manager shall provide the Owner with an accounting of all expenditures from the reserve fund in the monthly financial reports.
propertyManagement.s7Li2: Provide funds for necessary repairs and maintenance.
propertyManagement.s7Li3: Approve any repair or maintenance costs exceeding $500.00 before work is performed.
propertyManagement.s8Intro: The Property Manager shall provide the Owner with detailed annual reports, including but not limited to, the following:
propertyManagement.s8Li1: An income statement showing rental income, management fees, operating expenses, and any other deductions.
propertyManagement.s8Li2: A balance sheet showing the current financial status of the property.
propertyManagement.s8Li3: A statement of the reserve fund, including all contributions and expenditures.
propertyManagement.s8Li4: A summary of any significant events or issues that occurred during the calendar year.
propertyManagement.s8Footer: These reports shall be delivered to the Owner within 15 days after the end of each calendar year.
propertyManagement.s9Body: Either party may terminate this Agreement with 30 days' written notice. In the event of termination, the Property Manager shall be entitled to all management fees and reimbursement for expenses incurred up to the date of termination.
propertyManagement.s10Body: The Owner agrees to indemnify and hold the Property Manager harmless from any and all claims, losses, damages, liabilities, and expenses arising out of or related to the management of the Property, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the Property Manager.
propertyManagement.s11Body: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of TEXAS.
propertyManagement.s12Body: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements or understandings, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter herein.

tenantCriteria.title: Carwoods - Tenant Selection Criteria
tenantCriteria.heading: Tenant Selection Criteria
tenantCriteria.fairHousing: We appreciate your interest in our properties. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, familial status, national origin, disability, or other protected characteristics under the Texas Fair Housing Act.
tenantCriteria.allMust: All applicants must meet every requirement below. These standards are applied consistently to all applicants, including those using housing assistance (Section 8 or similar programs). Meeting some criteria does not qualify an applicant.
tenantCriteria.noFilters: No filters active — showing all sections
tenantCriteria.toc6a: When a guarantor may be considered
tenantCriteria.toc7b: When a co-signer may be considered
tenantCriteria.nonNeg1: All requirements below are mandatory. Applications that do not meet every requirement may be denied.
tenantCriteria.nonNeg2: No verbal assurances, explanations, or promises can replace documentation.
tenantCriteria.nonNeg3: Incomplete applications will not be processed.
tenantCriteria.nonNeg4: Housing assistance (Section 8) does not waive credit, background, employment, or rental history standards.
tenantCriteria.nonNeg5: Submitting an application does not guarantee approval.
tenantCriteria.glance1: Employment: 24+ months of verifiable employment history
tenantCriteria.glance2: Income: Gross monthly income ≥ 3× monthly rent (or ≥ 2.5× your tenant portion if using housing assistance)
tenantCriteria.glance3: Rental history: 24+ months of verifiable rental or mortgage payment history
tenantCriteria.glance5: Background: No criminal convictions, evictions, bankruptcies, or housing-related collections/negatives
tenantCriteria.emp1: Minimum 24 consecutive months of verifiable employment history
tenantCriteria.emp3: Recent job starts, short-term work, or unverified employment do not qualify
tenantCriteria.emp4: Self-employment requires 24 months of tax returns
tenantCriteria.inc1: Gross monthly income must be at least 3× the monthly rent
tenantCriteria.inc2: For housing assistance applicants, the income requirement is based on 2.5× your tenant portion of rent as determined by the Housing Authority
tenantCriteria.inc3: Income must be verifiable, recurring, and stable
tenantCriteria.inc4: Bank statements may be required to confirm cash-flow stability
tenantCriteria.rh1: Minimum 24 months of verifiable rental or mortgage payment history
tenantCriteria.rh2: Landlord references must be verifiable and responsive
tenantCriteria.rh4: Rental history must demonstrate on-time payments and proper care of the property
tenantCriteria.cred2b: Unpaid auto loan charge-offs, repossessions, or major delinquencies
tenantCriteria.cred2c: Pattern of unpaid obligations or excessive collections
tenantCriteria.cred3: While we welcome written explanations, rental decisions are based on verified credit reports and consistent criteria
tenantCriteria.bg4: No housing-related collections/negatives on the credit report
tenantCriteria.bg5: We do not deny applicants based on being a victim of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking, in compliance with Texas law
tenantCriteria.bg6: Inconsistencies between disclosures and screening results may result in denial
tenantCriteria.ha1: All applicants are screened using the same criteria for rental history, credit, criminal background, and references
tenantCriteria.ha2: Income requirement is 2.5× your tenant portion of rent as determined by the Housing Authority
tenantCriteria.ha3: Provide: valid voucher, RFTA packet, and your caseworker's contact information
tenantCriteria.ha4: No side payments; all rent amounts must be approved and documented by the Housing Authority
tenantCriteria.ha5: Voucher must be active and valid through the expected lease start date
tenantCriteria.ha6: Move-in requires Housing Authority inspection and rent reasonableness approval
tenantCriteria.creditExcIntro: Applicants who do not meet the minimum credit score requirement may be considered only if all conditions below are met. Approval under this exception is not guaranteed and remains at the sole discretion of the owner/agent.
tenantCriteria.ce1: 36+ months of verifiable, on-time rental history
tenantCriteria.ce2: No housing-related collections (utilities/energy/water/landlord claims) in the past 36 months
tenantCriteria.ce3: No unpaid auto loan charge-offs or repossessions in the past 48 months
tenantCriteria.ce4: Demonstrated positive bank balances and cash flow for the most recent 60 days
tenantCriteria.ce5: All other criteria (employment, income, background, references) must still be met
tenantCriteria.guarantorIntro: A guarantor is a qualified individual who agrees in writing to be financially responsible for the lease obligations if the tenant fails to pay or otherwise defaults. A guarantor is not a substitute for incomplete documentation and is not a "character reference." Guarantors are accepted only under the standards below, which are applied consistently to all applicants.
tenantCriteria.gwHeading: a. When a guarantor may be considered
tenantCriteria.gw1: A guarantor may be considered only when an applicant meets all screening requirements for credit, background, rental history, and documentation, but does not fully meet the income requirement.
tenantCriteria.gw2: A guarantor is not available to offset disqualifying items such as criminal convictions, evictions, bankruptcies, housing-related collections/negatives, or material misrepresentations.
tenantCriteria.gw3: Approval with a guarantor is not guaranteed and remains subject to owner/agent discretion based on documented risk factors and consistency with published standards.
tenantCriteria.gq1: Identity: Valid government-issued photo ID (color copy).
tenantCriteria.gq2: Income: Verifiable gross monthly income of at least 4× the monthly rent (for housing assistance applicants, 4× the full contract rent).
tenantCriteria.gq3: Employment: 24+ months of verifiable employment or self-employment history (self-employment requires 24 months of tax returns).
tenantCriteria.gq4: Credit: Minimum credit score of 700 (strictly enforced).
tenantCriteria.gq5: Credit disqualifiers: Housing-related collections (utilities/energy/water/landlord claims), repossessions, charge-offs, or a pattern of unpaid obligations may result in denial.
tenantCriteria.gq6: Background: No criminal convictions, evictions, or bankruptcies.
tenantCriteria.gq7: Residency: Guarantor must reside in the United States and be reachable for verification.
tenantCriteria.glHeading: c. Guarantor legal and payment terms
tenantCriteria.gl1: The guarantor must complete a separate application and pass screening. Application fees (if any) apply.
tenantCriteria.gl2: The guarantor must sign a guaranty agreement that provides joint and several liability for all lease obligations, including rent, fees, damages, and legal costs, to the fullest extent permitted by law.
tenantCriteria.gl3: The guarantor remains responsible for the lease term and any extensions/renewals unless released in writing by the owner/agent.
tenantCriteria.gl4: The presence of a guarantor does not change payment due dates or late fee policies.
tenantCriteria.gl5: At lease renewal, the tenant must independently meet all published income and qualification requirements, or the guarantor must agree in writing to remain in force for the renewal term. If neither condition is met, the lease may be non-renewed.
tenantCriteria.gn1: No side payments or undisclosed arrangements are permitted.
tenantCriteria.gn2: Documentation must be complete and verifiable. Incomplete guarantor packages will not be processed.
tenantCriteria.gn3: Carwoods reserves the right to request additional documentation to verify identity, income, and stability.
tenantCriteria.cosignerIntro: A co-signer is not the same as a guarantor. A co-signer signs the lease itself as a co-tenant and is jointly and severally liable for all lease obligations from the first day of the tenancy — regardless of whether the primary tenant pays. Because a co-signer is legally a co-tenant under Texas law, they are subject to the full applicant screening process and must independently meet every published qualification standard.
tenantCriteria.cdHeading: a. Key differences from a guarantor
tenantCriteria.cd1: Liability timing: A co-signer is liable from day one; a guarantor is only called upon after the primary tenant defaults.
tenantCriteria.cd2: Document signed: A co-signer signs the lease agreement itself; a guarantor signs a separate guaranty agreement.
tenantCriteria.cd3: Occupancy: A co-signer may or may not reside in the unit; a guarantor does not reside in the unit.
tenantCriteria.cd4: Qualification standard: A co-signer must meet all the same income, credit, background, employment, and rental history requirements as any primary applicant — there is no reduced standard.
tenantCriteria.cwHeading: b. When a co-signer may be considered
tenantCriteria.cw1: A co-signer may be considered only when the primary applicant meets all screening requirements for credit, background, rental history, and documentation, but does not fully meet the income requirement.
tenantCriteria.cw2: A co-signer is not available to offset disqualifying items such as criminal convictions, evictions, bankruptcies, housing-related collections/negatives, or material misrepresentations by the primary applicant.
tenantCriteria.cw3: Approval with a co-signer is not guaranteed and remains subject to owner/agent discretion.
tenantCriteria.cq1: Identity: Valid government-issued photo ID (color copy).
tenantCriteria.cq2: Income: Verifiable gross monthly income of at least 4× the monthly rent. Combined household income (co-signer + primary applicant) must still demonstrate the ability to sustain the full rent obligation.
tenantCriteria.cq3: Employment: 24+ months of verifiable employment or self-employment history (self-employment requires 24 months of tax returns).
tenantCriteria.cq4: Credit: Minimum credit score of 700 (strictly enforced).
tenantCriteria.cq5: Credit disqualifiers: Housing-related collections (utilities/energy/water/landlord claims), repossessions, charge-offs, or a pattern of unpaid obligations may result in denial.
tenantCriteria.cq6: Background: No criminal convictions, evictions, or bankruptcies.
tenantCriteria.cq7: Rental history: 24+ months of verifiable rental or mortgage payment history.
tenantCriteria.cq8: Residency: Co-signer must reside in the United States and be reachable for verification.
tenantCriteria.clHeading: d. Co-signer legal and payment terms
tenantCriteria.cl1: The co-signer must complete a full application and pass all screening. Application fees (if any) apply.
tenantCriteria.cl2: The co-signer signs the lease as a co-tenant and bears joint and several liability for all lease obligations — including rent, fees, damages, and legal costs — from the lease start date.
tenantCriteria.cl3: The co-signer's liability is not contingent on the primary tenant's default. Management may pursue the co-signer directly for any unpaid obligation without first exhausting remedies against the primary tenant.
tenantCriteria.cl4: The co-signer remains responsible for the full lease term and any extensions or renewals unless released in writing by the owner/agent.
tenantCriteria.cl5: At lease renewal, all co-signers must re-qualify under the then-current published standards, or the lease may be non-renewed.
tenantCriteria.cl6: The presence of a co-signer does not change payment due dates, late fee policies, or any other lease terms.
tenantCriteria.cn1: No side payments or undisclosed arrangements are permitted.
tenantCriteria.cn2: Documentation must be complete and verifiable. Incomplete co-signer packages will not be processed.
tenantCriteria.cn3: Carwoods reserves the right to request additional documentation to verify identity, income, and stability.
tenantCriteria.petsIntro: Pets are subject to separate approval. Restrictions may apply by type, breed, size, and quantity. Pet approval is not guaranteed even if all other criteria are met.
tenantCriteria.assistanceBody: Assistance animals (service animals and emotional support animals) are not pets. They are reasonable accommodations for disabilities under the Fair Housing Act. Breed, size, and species restrictions do not apply to assistance animals when properly documented. We evaluate assistance-animal requests in accordance with applicable law.

requiredDocs.title: Carwoods - Application Required Documents
requiredDocs.heading: Application Required Documents
requiredDocs.fairHousing: We appreciate your interest in our properties. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, familial status, national origin, disability, or other protected characteristics under the Texas Fair Housing Act.
requiredDocs.intro: All documents listed below are required for each adult applicant (18 years or older). Incomplete applications will not be processed. Providing documents does not guarantee approval. Documents are handled confidentially and used only for screening purposes. Please also review our
requiredDocs.id1: Valid government-issued photo ID (Driver's License or State ID, color copy).
requiredDocs.id2: Social Security Number verification (one of the following, color copy, showing full SSN):
requiredDocs.emp1: Most recent 90 days of pay stubs showing year-to-date earnings.
requiredDocs.emp2: Written employment verification (HR letter or email confirming start date, position, and current status).
requiredDocs.emp3: Bank statements for the most recent 60 days (original bank-exported PDF, all pages, must clearly show the applicant's full name and current address).
requiredDocs.emp4: Employer or HR contact information for verification (name, email, direct phone number).
requiredDocs.se1: Most recent two (2) years of filed federal tax returns.
requiredDocs.se2: Last 6 months of bank statements (original bank-exported PDF, all pages, must clearly show the applicant's full name and current address).
requiredDocs.se3: Business documentation (EIN letter, certificate of formation, or business license).
requiredDocs.rh1: Landlord reference information for the past 24 months (landlord name, email, and phone number).
requiredDocs.rh2: If no rental history but paying a mortgage, provide the last 24 months of mortgage payment history.
requiredDocs.petsLi: Pets (if applicable): Clear photos of each pet and current vaccination records.
requiredDocs.serviceAnimalLi: Service Animals: Under the Fair Housing Act and Texas Property Code, service animals are dogs (or miniature horses) individually trained to perform specific tasks directly related to a person's disability. We will not request documentation proving the animal is a certified or trained service animal, nor will we require the animal to demonstrate its task. However, we may ask:
requiredDocs.sa1: Whether the applicant has a disability-related need for the animal (yes/no only — we will not ask for the nature or extent of the disability).
requiredDocs.sa2: What specific task or work the animal has been trained to perform.
requiredDocs.saFooter: No pet deposit, pet fee, or pet rent may be charged for a service animal. The applicant remains liable for any damage caused by the animal beyond normal wear and tear.
requiredDocs.esaLi: Emotional Support Animals (ESA) and Other Assistance Animals: When the disability-related need for an ESA or other assistance animal is not obvious or known, we may request reliable documentation from a licensed healthcare professional (physician, psychiatrist, therapist, or other licensed mental health professional) currently treating the applicant. The letter must:
requiredDocs.esa1: Be written on the provider's official letterhead.
requiredDocs.esa2: Include the provider's name, license type, license number, state of licensure, and direct contact information.
requiredDocs.esa3: Confirm that the applicant has a disability (without disclosing the specific diagnosis).
requiredDocs.esa4: State that the animal provides disability-related support or therapeutic benefit.
requiredDocs.esa5: Be dated within the past 12 months.
requiredDocs.esaFooter: Online certificates, registry IDs, vest documentation, or letters from internet-based services are not accepted and do not constitute reliable documentation under HUD guidelines. No pet deposit, pet fee, or pet rent may be charged for an approved assistance animal. The applicant remains liable for damage caused by the animal beyond normal wear and tear.
requiredDocs.benefitsHeading: Applicants Receiving Government or Other Benefits
requiredDocs.va1: Current VA Benefits Award Letter (dated within the past 12 months) showing benefit type, monthly amount, and effective date.
requiredDocs.va2: Most recent 2 months of bank statements confirming VA deposit amounts.
requiredDocs.va3: DD-214 (Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty) is accepted as supplemental identity verification but is not required.
requiredDocs.ssaSsiLi: Social Security (SSA) or Supplemental Security Income (SSI):
requiredDocs.ssaSsi1: Current SSA Award Letter or Benefit Verification Letter (obtainable at ssa.gov) dated within the past 12 months, showing monthly benefit amount.
requiredDocs.ssaSsi2: Most recent 2 months of bank statements confirming SSA/SSI deposit amounts.
requiredDocs.ssdi1: Current SSDI Award Letter dated within the past 12 months, showing monthly benefit amount and disability onset date.
requiredDocs.ssdi2: Most recent 2 months of bank statements confirming SSDI deposit amounts.
requiredDocs.ret1: Most recent pension or retirement benefit statement (e.g., TCDRS, TRS, FERS, or private pension) dated within the past 12 months.
requiredDocs.ret2: Most recent 2 months of bank statements confirming deposit amounts.
requiredDocs.cs1: Court order or divorce decree showing the awarded amount and duration.
requiredDocs.cs2: Most recent 3 months of bank statements or OAG (Texas Office of the Attorney General) payment history confirming consistent receipt.
requiredDocs.otherBenefitsLi: All Other Benefits: Official award or benefit letter from the issuing agency (dated within the past 12 months) plus most recent 2 months of bank statements confirming deposit amounts.
requiredDocs.em1: Emergency contact for someone not residing in the household (full name, address, email, and phone number).
requiredDocs.s8Li1: Active Housing Choice Voucher showing tenant name, bedroom size, issue date, and expiration date.
requiredDocs.s8Li2: Tenant Rent Portion Estimate, affordability worksheet, or equivalent from the assigned caseworker or Housing Authority.
requiredDocs.s8Li3: Completed RFTA (Request for Tenancy Approval) packet (tenant sections).
requiredDocs.s8Li4: Caseworker's full name, email address, and direct phone number.
requiredDocs.s8Li5: Proof that household composition matches the voucher.
requiredDocs.s8Li6: Housing Authority inspection and rent reasonableness approval are required prior to move-in.
requiredDocs.s8Li7: HAP Contract and Housing Authority lease addendum are required after approval and inspection.
requiredDocs.s8Li8: Section 8 applicants are subject to the same credit, background, and rental history requirements as all other applicants. No side payments are permitted.
requiredDocs.guarantorIntro: A guarantor signs a separate guaranty agreement (not the lease) and is only liable if the primary tenant defaults. Guarantors are accepted only when requested by management — see
requiredDocs.g2: Social Security Number verification (SS card, W-2, 1099, or most recent tax return; showing full SSN).
requiredDocs.g3: Most recent 90 days of pay stubs showing year-to-date earnings.
requiredDocs.g4: Written employment verification (HR letter/email confirming start date, position, and current status).
requiredDocs.g5: Bank statements for the most recent 60 days (original bank-exported PDFs, all pages).
requiredDocs.g6: If self-employed: most recent 2 years of filed federal tax returns and last 6 months of bank statements.
requiredDocs.g7: Landlord reference information for the past 24 months (landlord name, email, and phone number). If no rental history but paying a mortgage, provide the last 24 months of mortgage payment history.
requiredDocs.g8: Signed guaranty agreement (provided by management; separate from the lease).
requiredDocs.cosignerIntro: A co-signer is not the same as a guarantor. A co-signer signs the lease itself as a co-tenant and is jointly and severally liable for all lease obligations from day one — regardless of whether the primary tenant pays. Co-signers must meet the full applicant qualification standards. See
requiredDocs.c2: Social Security Number verification (SS card, W-2, 1099, or most recent tax return; showing full SSN).
requiredDocs.c3: Most recent 90 days of pay stubs showing year-to-date earnings.
requiredDocs.c4: Written employment verification (HR letter/email confirming start date, position, and current status).
requiredDocs.c5: Bank statements for the most recent 60 days (original bank-exported PDFs, all pages).
requiredDocs.c6: If self-employed: most recent 2 years of filed federal tax returns and last 6 months of bank statements.
requiredDocs.c7: Landlord reference information for the past 24 months (landlord name, email, and phone number). If no rental history but paying a mortgage, provide the last 24 months of mortgage payment history.
requiredDocs.c8: Completed rental application and consent to full credit, background, and rental history screening.

pets.label: Yes — pet(s)
pets.desc: Cats, dogs, or other household pets

portalSetup.title: Carwoods - Portal Setup
portalSetup.metaDescription: Maintenance portal setup checks for API connectivity and authenticated access.
portalSetup.heading: Portal Home
portalSetup.intro: Open your portal tools from here, then use system checks when you need to troubleshoot setup or authentication.
portalSetup.portalActionsIntro: Available actions are shown based on your role after sign-in.
portalSetup.portalActionsUnavailable: Sign in to access requests and account tools.
portalSetup.guestBlocked: Guest accounts can view session information but cannot access portal tools.
portalSetup.systemChecksIntro: Use these diagnostics to validate API connectivity and authenticated portal access.
portalSetup.apiBaseMissing: Set VITE_API_BASE_URL in your root .env file to enable portal API checks.

errors.tokenRequired: Paste a bearer token before running this check.

portalAdminNotificationTest.metaDescription: Send test email, SMS, and in-app notifications to portal users (admin).
portalAdminNotificationTest.heading: Notification test
portalAdminNotificationTest.intro: Use these tools to verify delivery pipelines. Email and SMS create queued rows for your messaging provider to process; in-app notifications appear immediately in your portal inbox.
portalAdminNotificationTest.notAvailable: Sign in as an administrator with API access to use this page.

email.heading: Test email
email.hint: Queues an email delivery row with subject and body stored for the sender job.

sms.heading: Test SMS
sms.hint: Use E.164 format (leading + and country code). Queues an SMS delivery row for your messaging provider.

inApp.heading: Test in-app notification
inApp.hint: Creates an in-app notification for the selected portal user (admin, landlord, or tenant). The recipient sees it in the header bell and on the Notifications page.
inApp.recipientHelper: Choose any admin, landlord, or tenant to receive the test notification.

errors.unknown: Request failed. Try again or check your connection.
errors.invalid_phone_e164: Enter a valid E.164 phone number (for example +15551234567).
errors.forbidden: You do not have permission to run notification tests.

portalStatus.title: Carwoods - Portal Status
portalStatus.metaDescription: Portal troubleshooting checks for API connectivity and authentication health.
portalStatus.heading: Portal Status
portalStatus.intro: Troubleshooting page for portal diagnostics and session validation.

portalWorkspace.authRequired: Sign in from Portal Setup before using this workspace.
portalWorkspace.authUnconfigured: Configure authentication environment values before using portal workspaces.
portalWorkspace.authError: Unable to validate the saved portal session.
portalWorkspace.authForbidden: Your current session is not authorized for this workspace.

tenant.heading: Tenant Portal Workspace
tenant.intro: This route is reserved for tenant-focused maintenance features.
tenant.next1: Add authenticated tenant header and session guard.
tenant.next2: Implement requests list/create screens using portal APIs.
tenant.next3: Add attachment upload intent + message thread UI.

landlord.heading: Landlord Portal Workspace
landlord.intro: This route is reserved for landlord operations and request management.
landlord.next1: Add landlord access guard using /api/portal/me and role data.
landlord.next3: Add audit-friendly mutation flows and status updates.

admin.heading: Admin Portal Workspace
admin.intro: This route is reserved for admin-level controls and cross-account management.
admin.next1: Add admin-only controls for role assignment and policy settings.
admin.next2: Add admin-level reporting and export safeguards.
admin.next3: Add audit review tools for privileged actions.

portalProfile.title: Carwoods - Portal Profile
portalProfile.heading: Profile
portalProfile.intro: Update your account email, name, and phone number used for portal communications.
portalProfile.preferencesRefreshed: Your language and theme preferences have been updated from your saved settings.
portalProfile.guestBlocked: Guest accounts cannot edit profile details.
portalProfile.dataUnavailable: We could not load your profile data from the database. Please refresh and try again.

removePhotoConfirm.title: Remove profile photo?
removePhotoConfirm.body: Your profile picture will be removed. You can upload a new one anytime.

photoPreview.title: Adjust profile photo
photoPreview.hint: Drag the photo to reposition it. Use the slider or buttons to zoom in or out. The circle shows how your picture will appear.

photoErrors.invalidType: Choose a JPEG, PNG, or WebP image.
photoErrors.sourceTooLarge: Choose an image that is {{maxMb}} MB or smaller.
photoErrors.tooLarge: Image must be {{maxMb}} MB or smaller.
photoErrors.attachmentConfigUnavailable: Upload limits are not available. Try again later or contact support.
photoErrors.outputTooLarge: We could not shrink the photo enough. Try a simpler image.
photoErrors.storageUnavailable: Photo storage is not configured. Try again later.
photoErrors.uploadFailed: Could not update your photo. Please try again.

fields.notificationsSms: SMS notifications (I explicitly opt in to SMS notifications)
fields.notificationsSmsOptIn: I explicitly opt in to SMS notifications

smsOptInConfirm.title: Confirm SMS notification opt-in
smsOptInConfirm.body: Do you affirm that you explicitly opt in to SMS notifications for portal activity?

errors.signInRequired: Sign in before editing your profile.

errors.emailExists: That email is already in use by another user.
errors.phoneRequiredForSms: Mobile phone is required when SMS notifications are enabled.

portalNotificationPolicies.heading: Notification Policies
portalNotificationPolicies.intro: Manage per-property and per-request notification overrides for users.

overrides.heading: Existing overrides

errors.signInRequired: Sign in before managing notification policies.

portalRequests.title: Carwoods - Portal Requests
portalRequests.metaDescription: Create and manage maintenance requests, messages, and attachments.
portalRequests.heading: Maintenance Requests
portalRequests.intro: Use this workspace to create requests, manage request updates, and keep tenant and management communication in one thread.

errors.apiUnavailable: Set VITE_API_BASE_URL to enable request flows.
errors.guestBlocked: Guest accounts cannot use maintenance request flows.
errors.noTenantLeaseAccess: No active tenant lease was found for your account.
errors.noTenantLeaseAccessWithContactPrefix: No active tenant lease was found for your account. Please contact your property manager {{name}} at
errors.noTenantLeaseAccessWithContact: No active tenant lease was found for your account. Please contact {{name}} at {{email}}.
errors.attachmentStorageUnavailable: Attachment uploads are temporarily unavailable. Please try again later or contact support.
errors.attachmentUploadFailed: We couldn't upload this file right now. Please try again in a moment.
errors.messageProtected: This message cannot be deleted because it is referenced by Elsa history.
errors.uploadIntentMissingPath: Upload intent did not return a storage path.
errors.unsupportedFileType: File type not supported. Allowed: images and videos only.
errors.fileTooLarge: File exceeds the {{maxMb}} MB limit for this type.
errors.attachmentLimitExceeded: You can upload up to 3 attachments per request.
errors.videoTooLong: Video duration exceeds the {{maxSeconds}} second limit.
errors.videoMetadataFailed: We could not read this video's duration. Try another file.

list.heading: Request list
list.empty: No requests found for this account.
list.selectPrompt: Select a request to view details.
list.noMatchesFilter: No requests match the current filters.

management.heading: Management updates
management.statusCodePlaceholder: NOT_STARTED, ACKNOWLEDGED, SCHEDULED, WAITING_ON_TENANT, WAITING_ON_VENDOR, COMPLETE, CANCELLED

messages.heading: Message thread
messages.composeHelper: Use the toolbar to format text. What you see is what others see after you send. Links must use https://, http://, or mailto:. Maximum {{max}} characters.
messages.composeLinkInvalidUrl: Use a link that starts with https://, http://, or mailto:.
messages.deleteConfirmBody: This will permanently remove the message from the thread.

attachments.heading: Attachments
attachments.instructions: Upload photos or short videos only. Max 3 files per request. Images up to 10 MB each. Videos up to 50 MB and 10 seconds each. Drag and drop or choose files.
attachments.linkPromptFallback: Attachment link ready. Copy it from the prompt.
attachments.shareUnavailable: Unable to create a share link right now.
attachments.sharePrompt: Copy and share this temporary link:
attachments.sharePromptFallback: Share link generated. Copy it from the prompt.
attachments.deepLinkOpenFailed: We could not open that attachment link. Sign in with an account that can access this request and try again.
attachments.deleteConfirmBody: This permanently removes the attachment from the request.

cancel.heading: Cancel request
cancel.description: You can cancel this request because it has not yet been scheduled. This cannot be undone.
cancel.confirmBody: This will mark the request as cancelled. Your property manager will be notified. You can submit a new request at any time.

elsa.heading: AI Assistant
elsa.summaryFallbackNote: The AI model was unavailable, so this is a quick excerpt from the request instead.
elsa.summaryEmpty: No summary was returned. Try again in a moment.

copyDialog.title: Suggested message copied
copyDialog.body: The AI suggested message has been copied into the message box. Dismiss this suggestion now?

audit.heading: Audit log
audit.empty: No audit events for this request.

harSearch.heading: Look up a HAR listing
harSearch.harIdHelperText: Paste the full HAR.com homedetail URL or just the numeric ID at the end
harSearch.invalidInput: Could not find a numeric listing ID in the value you entered. Paste the full HAR.com URL (e.g. https://www.har.com/homedetail/…/8469293) or just the numeric ID.
harSearch.notFound: No listing data found for that ID. Check the ID and try again.
harSearch.noApiConfigured: API is not configured. Set VITE_API_BASE_URL in your .env to enable HAR search.
harSearch.fetchError: Could not reach HAR. The listing may be unavailable, or try entering details manually.
harSearch.accessDenied: HAR.com blocked the request (HTTP 403). Try again in a few minutes, or enter the listing details manually.
harSearch.unexpectedResponse: The server returned an OK response but no listing payload. Try again, or enter details manually. If this keeps happening, check that the site and API versions match.

form.heading: Property details
